catalysts.thesis: The FDA lifting the CRL for deramiocel drastically de-risks the asset, while the new August 22 PDUFA and Priority Review set a clear regulatory runway for approval in Duchenne muscular dystrophy.
catalysts.catalyst_details: On March 11, 2026, it was reported that the FDA lifted a previous Complete Response Letter (CRL) and granted a new PDUFA date of August 22, 2026, following Capricor's resubmission of Phase 3 HOPE-3 data.
catalysts.absorption_status: The stock gained 9% on Tuesday (March 10) immediately following the news, but the complete reversal of a CRL typically leads to a multi-week re-rating. It is not up >8% today.
catalysts.relative_volume: Elevated above 30-day average following the regulatory update.
catalysts.prediction_market: High implied probability of FDA approval given the rare reversal of a CRL and positive Phase 3 data.
catalysts.recency_proof: March 11, 2026 BioSpace article detailing the CRL lift and August PDUFA.
catalysts.risk: Regulatory risk remains as the FDA could still issue another CRL or delay the decision.
catalysts.expected_upside: 25-40% run-up into the August PDUFA date.
catalysts.mispricing_evidence: At a $1.8B market cap, a potentially disease-modifying Duchenne therapy with a lifted CRL and Priority Review warrants a much higher premium.
catalysts.x_sentiment: Biotech investors and analysts are expressing strong optimism over the FDA's sudden reversal.
catalysts.hold_time_estimate: 2-4 Weeks for momentum trend, or hold until August 2026 PDUFA.
